home.hero_title: The Joy of Paddling in Trondheim
home.hero_sub: Trondhjems Kajakklubb — an active community for all levels since 1932
home.disciplines_title: What do you want to paddle?
home.join_text: For NOK 800 per year you get access to equipment, tours, courses and an active social community in one of Norway's largest kayak clubs.

disciplines.hav_desc: ~450 members, weekly tours in the Trondheim fjord and major summer expeditions.
disciplines.elv_desc: Training on Nidelva and trips to Sjoa, Trysil and Oppdal for all levels.
disciplines.flattvann_desc: Wednesday training on Jonsvannet — competitive paddling for speed enthusiasts.
disciplines.surfski_desc: Open cockpit, wave riding and high speed. Established 2023 with dedicated instructors.
disciplines.polo_desc: Team sport with kayak and ball. National gold medal 2016. Active since 2010.
disciplines.junior_desc: For children and youth aged 11–16. Training year-round on water and in Pirbadet.

events.title: Events & Courses
events.no_events: No upcoming events in this category.

membership.title: Membership
membership.how_text: Register via Min Idrett, pay the membership fee and accept the club's bylaws. You are then a member!
membership.included_equipment: Free loan of kayak and equipment for club activities
membership.included_tours: Access to all tours and activities
membership.qual_text: For sea, river and surfski paddling, an NPF basic course (16 hours, wet card) is required before borrowing equipment or joining tours. Flat water, polo and junior can start without a course but must paddle with an instructor until qualified.
membership.after_text: Submit a photo of your wet card for approval and register in Padleboken before joining tours or borrowing equipment.

hav.title: Sea Kayaking
hav.intro: In the sea kayaking group, enjoyment and adventure are the focus. With ~450 members we offer something for everyone — from a relaxed Sunday tour to week-long expeditions along the Norwegian coast.
hav.local_title: Local paddling in the Trondheim Fjord
hav.local_text: We register around 3,000 paddling trips per year from two locations. Skansen is home base with the historic clubhouse, loan kayaks and a strong social community. Østmarkneset at Ladekaia offers paddling in beautiful surroundings next to a popular restaurant. Both locations have equipment available for members to borrow.
hav.weekly_text: Tuesday paddling at 18:00 is open to beginners and those trying sea kayaking for the first time. The Sunday tour at 12:00 is a tradition with a lunch stop and social time — suitable for all levels. Both tours are organised by volunteers and are included in the membership.
hav.expedition_text: Every year we arrange ambitious summer trips to destinations such as Lofoten, Kragerø, Helgeland and Bohuslän in Sweden. In 2022 we completed twelve trips and a paddling festival. Tours vary in length and difficulty so there is always something for you — whether you want your first multi-day tour or wish to navigate demanding waters.
hav.equipment_text: As a member you can borrow kayaks, paddles, skirts, life jackets and other necessary equipment for all club activities at no extra cost. Kayaks are available for all sizes and abilities. It is also possible to rent a storage space for your own kayak at Skansen or Østmarkneset (waiting list applies).
hav.safety_text: An NPF basic course (16 hours, wet card) is required to join tours and borrow equipment. The course gives you fundamental paddling technique, capsize rescue skills and sea safety knowledge. TKK runs its own courses and technique courses throughout the season.

elv.title: River Kayaking
elv.intro: River kayaking at TKK is about excitement, nature experiences and great camaraderie. From the gentle currents of Nidelva to the pulsating rapids at Sjoa — there is something for every taste and skill level.
elv.local_text: The river Nidelva runs through the heart of Trondheim and is our natural training ground. The river offers fine stretches for beginners, and we hold weekly paddling sessions here throughout the season. Few cities in Norway can offer river paddling right in the city centre.
elv.trips_text: We arrange planned and spontaneous trips to some of Norway's best rivers: Sjoa with its classic rapids, Trysil with varied difficulty, gentle stretches near Oppdal and more advanced paddling northward towards Nordland. Trips are open to all experience levels and we always ensure groups are well matched.
elv.winter_text: When the river freezes, we move training indoors to Pirbadet. Here we work on technique, Eskimo rolls and balance in a safe and warm environment. Pirbadet sessions are a social highlight of the season and a perfect place to learn rolling from scratch.
elv.slalom_text: TKK has dedicated slalom kayaks and holds regular slalom sessions. Slalom is a great way to build technical precision paddling and gives you a strong foundation for tackling demanding water.
elv.equipment_text: We loan river kayaks and all necessary safety equipment — helmet, life jacket and skirt — to members for training and touring. A basic river kayaking course is required to borrow equipment and join tours.

flattvann.title: Flat Water
flattvann.intro: The flat water group is a revival of a proud competitive tradition at TKK — after more than 40 years dormant, competitive paddling is back. For those who love speed, technique and pushing their limits.
flattvann.training_text: Throughout summer we gather at Jonsvannet every Wednesday at 18:00 for structured training. Jonsvannet is a calm and beautiful lake outside Trondheim and provides perfect conditions for speed work and technique.
flattvann.about_text: Flat water paddling is about moving fast and efficiently on calm water in specially built kayak hulls designed for minimal water resistance. The discipline features in the Olympic Games and is one of the technically most demanding branches of paddling. At TKK the threshold is low — we welcome everyone who is curious, regardless of experience.
flattvann.boats_text: Flat water and surfski kayaks are radically different from the sea kayaks you may be used to. Narrow, long and lightning fast — but they require balance and technique. The club has flat water boats available for members to borrow for training.

surfski.title: Surfski
surfski.intro: Surfski is the perfect combination of sea kayaking range and flat water speed — with the extra dimension of wave riding. Established at TKK in June 2023 and growing fast.
surfski.about_text: A surfski is a long, narrow and open kayak originally developed in South Africa for ocean paddling in waves. The open cockpit makes it safe to capsize and remount, and the self-draining design means no water accumulates. At 10–15 kg for composite models they are light to transport and easy to handle.
surfski.speed_text: Surfski is designed to exploit wind and waves — the technique is called 'downwinding' and involves surfing from crest to crest on waves, letting natural forces carry you forward. It is a unique and exhilarating experience that combines fitness and technical demands in a singular way.
surfski.training_text: TKK has three certified surfski instructors and club surfskis available for qualified members to borrow. Courses run from May to September with limited places — register early. Basic training following NPF's progression system is mandatory before borrowing equipment unsupervised.
surfski.start_text: Beginners should start on wider and more stable models. As technique and balance develop you can move to narrower and faster hulls. The most important thing is not to give up after the first capsize — it is part of the process! Contact group leader Torleif for more information.

polo.title: Kayak Polo
polo.intro: Kayak polo is a team sport where two teams attempt to score goals by throwing a ball into the opponent's hanging net — from a kayak. It is intense, tactical and incredibly fun. TKK has had an active polo team since 2010 and won national gold in 2016.
polo.about_text: Kayak polo is played on a defined water area with two teams of five players. The ball is thrown with hands or a paddle blade — but you must always remain seated in the kayak. The sport demands a combination of paddling technique, coordination, team tactics and quick decision-making. There is nothing quite like it.
polo.training_text: In summer we train outdoors at Kyvannet, a small and pleasant lake just outside Trondheim. In winter we move to Pirbadet for indoor sessions. Training is open to all interested parties — no kayak experience is required to come and try! We have equipment for beginners.
polo.competition_text: TKK participates in Norwegian and international tournaments. The highlight is the Norwegian championship (NM) where TKK has regularly featured at the top. Representing the club in competition is a source of pride for all polo players at TKK.
polo.join_text: Curious? Come to a training session — no experience required. We have the kayaks and equipment ready. Contact us via the Facebook group 'Kajakkpolo i Trondheim' or call the group leader.

junior.title: Junior
junior.intro: The junior programme is one of TKK's most popular offerings. For children and young people aged 11–16 we provide a safe and active environment where paddling, play and nature experiences go hand in hand.
junior.about_text: We believe in letting young people experience nature from a unique perspective — from a kayak. The junior programme emphasises safety, mastery and community. All instructors hold background clearance and have experience working with children and young people.
junior.summer_text: Tuesday training at Skansen, Kyvannet and Nidelva. The focus is on river paddling with gradual progression — from still water to easy rapids. We always adapt training to participants' level and confidence. Flat water options are available for those who prefer it.
junior.winter_text: Saturday training at Pirbadet. Here we work on basic technique, balance and Eskimo rolls in the pool. Pirbadet sessions are always popular and a great place to get to know other young paddlers in a safe environment.
junior.trips_text: We organise trips and courses specially designed for children, youth and families throughout the season. The trips are designed to suit families with different experience levels and are a wonderful way to introduce paddling to the whole family.
junior.price: Children's membership: only NOK 50 per year

about.title: About the Club
about.intro: Trondhjems Kajakklubb is one of Norway's largest kayak clubs with ~500 members and six active disciplines.
about.values_text: Enjoyment, adventure and inclusion. We look after each other on the water and on land.

klubben.title: The club
klubben.intro: Everything about Trondhjems Kajakklubb — history, administration, club house and more.
klubben.admin_intro: Here you will find governing documents, protocols from annual meetings and board meetings, and other administrative information about the club.
klubben.admin_annual_text: The annual meeting is the club's highest authority and is held every year in the first quarter. Here the future plan is adopted, the budget approved and the board elected. Protocols from recent annual meetings are available on request — contact the board.
klubben.admin_board_text: The board meets regularly throughout the year to lead the club's day-to-day operations, finances and health & safety. Board meeting minutes are archived and available to members on request.
klubben.admin_bylaws_text: TKK's bylaws are the governing document for the club's activities. The business plan for 2026–2028 was adopted at the 2026 annual meeting and sets the direction for strategy, preservation and budgeting during the period.
klubben.admin_contact: Contact the board for access to documents.
klubben.clubhouse_intro: TKK has two bases along the Trondheim Fjord with access to water, equipment storage and social facilities.
klubben.clubhouse_skansen_text: The clubhouse at Skansen was opened June 26, 1953 and is the home of Trondhjems Kajakklubb. Here you will find the loan kayaks, storage and social areas. The address is Nedre Ila 12, 7018 Trondheim.
klubben.clubhouse_ostmark_text: Østmarkneset at Ladekaia is TKK's second base with kayak equipment for loan and a fantastic location right by the water and a popular restaurant.
klubben.clubhouse_storage_text: Members can rent a storage space for their own kayak at both bases. 2026 prices: Skansen NOK 750, Østmarkneset NOK 600. There is currently a waiting list.
klubben.social_intro: The social group works for enjoyment and community among club members through events and spontaneous activities throughout the year.
klubben.social_traditions_text: May 1: AVPLASK marks the start of the season. First Sunday of Advent: Advent paddling. Autumn: Crab evenings. Before Christmas: Lutefisk dinner.
klubben.social_spontaneous_text: Throughout summer, breakfast paddles are organised on warm mornings — often to Munkholmen. In autumn and winter, evening and moonlight tours depart from Skansen.
klubben.social_facebook_text: Updates about spontaneous tours and events are posted in the club's Facebook group.
klubben.support_intro: TKK has two adopted support schemes for members.
klubben.support_edu_text: Support for members wishing to take relevant courses or training in paddling and safety. Applications are sent to the board with a description of the course, cost and relevance to the club's activities.
klubben.support_comp_text: TKK supports young paddlers who wish to participate in national or international competitions. The scheme applies to members under 25. Applications are sent to the board.
klubben.support_apply: Applications are sent to the board by email or submitted in writing at the clubhouse.
klubben.refund_intro: TKK reimburses mileage and certain expenses for volunteers and elected officials carrying out assignments for the club.
klubben.refund_rate_text: The rate follows the Norwegian government travel regulations. The journey must be pre-approved by the board or the relevant group leader. Claim forms are available at the clubhouse or from the treasurer.
klubben.refund_other_text: Receipts for expenses incurred on behalf of the club must be submitted to the treasurer within 30 days. Missing receipts may result in the reimbursement not being granted.

hms.title: H&S
hms.plan_intro: Trondhjems Kajakklubb (TKK) is a sports club with approx. 500 members. The club's goal is to avoid incidents that lead to harm to people, the environment or equipment.
hms.org_title: Organisation and responsibility for activity leadership
hms.org_text: The club leader holds overall responsibility for all safety tasks being addressed and delegated. All paddling activities require a responsible leader with sufficient competence, assessed according to the Norwegian Paddling Federation's wet card ladder. Written risk assessments are required before activities.
hms.children_title: Guidelines for activity leaders with children
hms.children_text: Coaches, instructors and activity leaders staying overnight with children must not share a room alone with a child. There must always be a minimum of two adults present on overnight trips with minors. Parents/guardians must know who is responsible for their child. Digital communication must take place via open club channels.
hms.incident_text: Learning from accidents and incidents is a very important part of H&S work and the threshold for reporting should be low. All incidents must be reported and handled by the board. In the event of serious accidents the club leader must be notified immediately.
hms.environment_title: Leave no trace and nature conservation
hms.environment_text: Club members are expected to understand that certain paddling activities can have a negative impact on the environment. The right to roam gives us rights we should appreciate, but with accompanying duties. Particular attention is required during nesting season.
hms.equipment_text: Club equipment used in organised activities and borrowed by members for their own activities must be regularly inspected and maintained by group leaders. Damaged equipment must be marked and taken out of use immediately. First aid equipment is maintained by the house manager (checked twice yearly). VHF radio is required for tour leaders.
hms.emergency_text: Call 112/113 or use VHF channel 16 in emergencies. Automatic position reporting is recommended. Club leadership must be notified once the situation is under control. The emergency plan describes the measures to be implemented to reduce the consequences of an incident.
hms.background_text: A background check certificate is required for all persons aged 16 or over who will work with minors or vulnerable persons in the club. See the dedicated page for the procedure.
hms.revision_text: The H&S plan is revised annually at the first board meeting after the annual general meeting.
hms.generelt_intro: The internal control system ensures that the club's H&S work is systematic, documented and continuously improved.
hms.generelt_text:
TKK follows the requirements of the Internal Control Regulations. Internal control covers all organised activities and use of the club's facilities and equipment. Responsibility for internal control lies with the board, but all elected officials and activity leaders share responsibility for H&S in practice.

Internal control includes:
• Mapping and assessment of risk factors
• Written procedures for high-risk activities
• Training of activity leaders
• Systematic recording and follow-up of incidents
• Regular revision of the H&S plan
hms.hav_intro: Guidelines and risk assessment for organised sea kayaking under Trondhjems Kajakklubb.
hms.hav_competence_text: All participants in group sea paddling must have approved paddling competence — Basic Sea Course or equivalent (wet card). Tour leaders assess competence level and may refuse participants who do not meet requirements.
hms.hav_collective_text: Everyone shares collective responsibility for the group's safety. No one should paddle alone and the group must stay together. Sunday tours start at 12:00 from Skansen with planned routes towards destinations such as Munkholmen.
hms.hav_traffic_text: Crossing the speedboat lane (20–37 knots) must be done together as a group at Ravnkloutløpet. Tour leaders must continuously monitor boat traffic and warn the group about nearby vessels.
hms.hav_risk_text: Four primary risks are identified: physical injury, drowning, hypothermia and medical conditions. Preventive measures: approved paddling competence, waterproof clothing, equipped kayak with skirt, weather monitoring and awareness of boat traffic.
hms.hav_emergency_text: In the event of an incident: provide first aid and contact emergency services (113 medical, 112 police). The incident must be reported to club leadership. Use VHF channel 16 for maritime emergency communication.
hms.elv_intro: Guidelines and risk assessment for organised river kayaking on Nidelva and other rivers.
hms.elv_nidelva_text: Nidelva is TKK's primary river paddling arena. Weekly training sessions are held along the river throughout the season. All participants must have minimum basic river kayaking competence. Tour leaders are responsible for risk assessment before and during paddling.
hms.elv_risk_text: Primary risks in river kayaking: entrapment in current, rapids and obstacles, hypothermia and drowning. Requirements: helmet and life jacket are mandatory. All kayaks must have fore and aft buoyancy. The group must never spread out so that control is lost.
hms.elv_spontaneous_text: For spontaneous trips with borrowed TKK equipment: the tour leader is responsible for ensuring all participants have sufficient competence and that the equipment is in order. The trip is registered in Padleboken. If in doubt about competence or conditions, cancel the trip.
hms.varsel_intro: Speak up when you experience something that violates our regulations, ethical guidelines and policies. We want the threshold for reporting to be low!
hms.varsel_what_text: You can report incidents you have experienced, witnessed or heard about: abuse, violence, embezzlement, harassment, bullying, cheating in competitions, racist remarks, discriminatory behaviour.
hms.varsel_how_text: Reporting is done via the Norwegian Sports Federation's whistleblowing portal. You can also contact the TKK board directly if you have questions about the procedure.
hms.hendelser_intro: The board welcomes reports about all paddling-related incidents, including accidents, near-misses and other events from which the club can learn.
hms.hendelser_how_text: Use the reporting form below. The report is sent directly to the board and handled at the next board meeting. In the event of serious accidents, the club leader must be contacted directly.
hms.hendelser_previous_2024: Capsize on group tour to Knarrlaget — April 2024
hms.politiattest_intro: A background check certificate (politiattest) is required for coaches, instructors and leaders who will have direct contact with minors (under 18) or people with developmental disabilities.
hms.politiattest_who_text: Coaches, instructors and team leaders who will have direct contact with minors (persons under 18) or people with developmental disabilities are always covered. The certificate must be renewed every three years when taking on a new role.
hms.politiattest_how_text:
1. Contact TKK's background check coordinator with details of your role.
2. The coordinator confirms the purpose digitally and sends instructions for application via Min idrett.
3. The certificate is shown to the coordinator — digital presentation via Digipost is accepted.

pirbadet.title: Pirbadet
pirbadet.intro: TKK holds regular training sessions at Pirbadet throughout the winter season — from mid-October to the end of April (excluding Easter week). All groups train here: technique and rolling for sea kayaking, kayak polo and junior.
pirbadet.teknikk_text: Wednesdays 21:00–22:30 in the wave pool. Open to all TKK members — no registration required. Max 12 kayaks in the water; max 6 when the wave machine operates. Changing rooms open 30 min before and close 30 min after. Everyone must leave the building by 23:00.
pirbadet.polo_text: Thursdays 21:00–22:30 in the deep section of the sports pool. Open to all TKK members. Registration via the Spond app is mandatory.
pirbadet.junior_text: Saturdays 19:00–20:30 in the wave pool. For members under 20. Guests from partner clubs are welcome. No registration required.
pirbadet.rules_text: All equipment must be rinsed thoroughly with fresh water after training. When the wave machine is operating, one person must monitor the emergency stop button at all times.

auth.register_intro: Use the same email address you registered with in Minidrett/NIF.
auth.email_hint: Must match your registered email in Minidrett.no
auth.check_inbox_desc: We have sent a verification link to your email address. Click the link to activate your account.
auth.verification_sent: We have sent a verification link to {email}. Click the link in the email to activate your account.
auth.redirecting: You will be redirected in a moment…
auth.link_expired_desc: The verification link is no longer valid. Please register again.
auth.verification_failed_desc: The verification link is invalid. Please try registering again.
auth.login_intro: Log in with your email address and password.
auth.password_too_short: Password must be at least 8 characters.
auth.email_taken: This email address is already registered.
auth.email_send_failed: Could not send verification email. Please try again.
auth.email_not_verified_error: You must verify your email address before you can log in.
auth.generic_error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
auth.nif_consent_intro: To link your account to your TKK membership, we fetch your details from the Norwegian Sports Federation (NIF) via Minidrett.no. Your email address is the key for the lookup.
auth.nif_consent_disclaimer: The information is stored only to identify you as a TKK member and is shown on your profile. It is not shared with third parties.
auth.decline_warning: If you decline, your account will be deleted immediately.
auth.redirecting_to_profile: You will be redirected to your profile…
auth.nif_not_found_desc: We could not find an active TKK registration linked to your email address in NIF. Contact the board if you believe this is an error.
auth.nif_not_linked: Not linked to NIF. Go to NIF consent to link your account.
